diagrams.title: OS Architecture Layers
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchical layering of operating system components from hardware to user applications
elements: HARDWARE (CPU, Memory, I/O Devices)
KERNEL (Process Management, Memory Management, Device Management, File System)
SYSTEM CALL INTERFACE (API between user and kernel)
SYSTEM UTILITIES (Compilers, Editors, Shells)
USER APPLICATIONS (Word Processors, Browsers, Games)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the abstraction hierarchy and how each layer provides services to the layer above it

diagrams.title: System Call Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Visual representation of how user programs request kernel services through system calls
elements: USER PROGRAM calls read()
C LIBRARY (libc) prepares parameters
TRAP/SOFTWARE INTERRUPT triggered
CPU switches from USER MODE to KERNEL MODE
KERNEL validates request and permissions
KERNEL executes actual I/O operation
Return result through kernel to library
Control returns to user program in USER MODE
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the mechanism of transitioning from unprivileged to privileged mode for protected operations

diagrams.title: Monolithic vs Microkernel Architecture
diagrams.description: Comparison of two major OS kernel design philosophies showing component organization
elements: MONOLITHIC: Single kernel block containing all services (Process Schedular, Memory Manager, File System, Device Drivers) running in kernel space
MICROKERNEL: Small central kernel (IPC, Basic Scheduling, Basic Memory) with separate user-space servers (File System Server, Device Driver Server, Network Server) communicating via message passing
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the structural differences between monolithic and microkernel approaches and their trade-offs

diagrams.title: Process Communication via System Calls
diagrams.description: Shows how user processes interact with kernel for resource access and inter-process communication
elements: USER PROCESS 1 requests service via system call
KERNEL receives request in kernel mode
Kernel performs operation (reads file, allocates memory, etc.)
Result returned to process
Multiple processes can make system calls concurrently
Kernel acts as resource manager coordinating access
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how the kernel mediates all resource access and maintains system integrity
